title: RIOT Hash Rate Expansion and Operational Efficiency Metrics
text: Hash rate expansion has been a primary driver of RIOT's operational growth across 2024-2026, though with notable guidance revisions reflecting facility expansion delays. The company achieved 154% hash rate growth in 2024, reaching 31.5 exahash per second by year-end, despite the April halving event. For Q3 2024, deployed hash rate reached 28 exahash, up 159% year-over-year. However, these impressive growth metrics were followed by guidance revisions in Q3 2024, with the company reducing 2024 expectations from 36.3 to 34.9 exahash and 2025 expectations from 56.6 to 46.7 exahash due to Kentucky facility delays. In Q1 2025, the company deployed 33.7 exahash of capacity, demonstrating continued expansion. By Q2 2025, hash rate utilization reached 87% from 61% in the prior year, indicating improved facility utilization rates. Full-year 2025 guidance targeted ending the year at approximately 46.7 exahash, which was achieved through a combination of new equipment deployment and acquired capacity from Rhodium operations. The company has maintained industry-leading power costs averaging 3.0-3.5 cents per kilowatt-hour, despite the challenging competitive environment. Future hash rate guidance appears moderated, with management targeting approximately 22% growth in 2025, reflecting realistic deployment timelines and equipment availability constraints.
